```kotlin
// Helper function to check if array B is a cyclic rotation of array A (assuming lengths are equal)
fun isCyclicRotation(A: LongArray, B: LongArray): Boolean {
    val k = A.size
    if (k == 0) return true
    if (k == 1) return A[0] == B[0]

    // Create A concatenated with itself (conceptually or physically)
    // We check if B matches any rotation of A.
    for (start in 0 until k) {
        var match = true
        for (i in 0 until k) {
            if (B[i] != A[(start + i) % k]) {
                match = false
                break
            }
        }
        if (match) return true
    }
    return false
}

fun solve(nums: Array<Long>): Long {
    val n = nums.size
    if (n == 0) return 0L

    // 1. Calculate S (sorted version of nums)
    val s = nums.sortedArray()

    // 2. Find divisors K of N
    val divisors = mutableListOf<Int>()
    var i = 1
    while (i * i <= n) {
        if (n % i == 0) {
            divisors.add(i)
            if (i * i != n) {
                divisors.add(n / i)
            }
        }
        i++
    }

    var totalSum: Long = 0

    // 3. Loop through k in divisors
    for (k in divisors) {
        var isSortable = true
        
        // Iterate over blocks
        var blockIndex = 0
        while (blockIndex * k < n) {
            val start = blockIndex * k
            val end = start + k
            
            // Extract C_i (from nums) and S_i (from s)
            // Since we are using array slices, let's copy the relevant parts.
            
            // C_i: nums[start]...nums[end-1]
            val c_i = LongArray(k) { nums[start + it] }
            // S_i: s[start]...s[end-1]
            val s_i = LongArray(k) { s[start + it] }
            
            // Condition 1: Multiset equality check.
            // Since S_i is already sorted, we sort C_i and compare.
            val c_i_sorted = c_i.sortedArray()
            
            if (!c_i_sorted.contentEquals(s_i)) {
                isSortable = false
                break
            }

            // Condition 2: Cyclic rotation check.
            // If multisets match, check if S_i is a cyclic rotation of C_i.
            // This check is necessary because S_i is sorted, while C_i might not be.
            if (k > 1) {
                if (!isCyclicRotation(c_i, s_i)) {
                    isSortable = false
                    break
                }
            } 
            // If k=1, C_i=[a], S_i=[a]. They match if multiset check passed, and rotation is trivial.
            
            blockIndex++
        }

        if (isSortable) {
            totalSum += k.toLong()
        }
    }

    return totalSum
}
```

```kotlin
// Helper functions for KMP (LPS array computation and search)

fun computeLPS(pattern: Array<Long>): IntArray {
    val M = pattern.size
    val lps = IntArray(M)
    var length = 0 // length of the previous longest prefix suffix
    var i = 1
    while (i < M) {
        if (pattern[i] == pattern[length]) {
            length++
            lps[i] = length
            i++
        } else {
            if (length != 0) {
                length = lps[length - 1]
            } else {
                lps[i] = 0
                i++
            }
        }
    }
    return lps
}

fun KMPSearch(text: Array<Long>, pattern: Array<Long>): Boolean {
    val N = text.size
    val M = pattern.size
    if (M == 0) return true
    if (N < M) return false

    val lps = computeLPS(pattern)

    var i = 0 // index for text
    var j = 0 // index for pattern

    while (i < N) {
        if (pattern[j] == text[i]) {
            i++
            j++
        }
        if (j == M) {
            // Found a match. Since we only care if it exists, we return true.
            // The match ends at i-1.
            return true
            // If we needed all occurrences, we'd reset j = lps[j-1] and continue.
        } else if (i < N && pattern[j] != text[i]) {
            if (j != 0) {
                j = lps[j - 1]
            } else {
                i++
            }
        }
    }
    return false
}

// Function to check if B is a cyclic permutation of A
fun isCyclicPermutation(A: Array<Long>, B: Array<Long>): Boolean {
    val k = A.size
    if (k == 0) return true
    if (A.size != B.size) return false // Should not happen based on problem setup

    if (k == 1) return true

    // Construct T = A + A. We need T to be an Array<Long>
    val TA = Array(2 * k) { 0L }
    for (i in 0 until k) {
        TA[i] = A[i]
        TA[i + k] = A[i]
    }
    
    // Search for B in TA. If found, B is a cyclic permutation of A.
    return KMPSearch(TA, B)
}


fun solve(nums: Array<Long>): Long {
    val n = nums.size
    
    // 1. Calculate S, the sorted version of nums.
    val sortedNums = nums.sortedArray()

    var totalSum: Long = 0

    // 2. Find all divisors k of n.
    val divisors = mutableListOf<Int>()
    var d = 1
    while (d * d <= n) {
        if (n % d == 0) {
            divisors.add(d)
            if (d * d != n) {
                divisors.add(n / d)
            }
        }
        d++
    }

    // 3. Check each divisor k
    for (k in divisors) {
        var isSortable = true
        val m = n / k // Number of blocks

        // 4. Iterate through blocks i = 0 to m-1
        for (i in 0 until m) {
            val start = i * k
            
            // i. Define the original block A_i
            val A_i = Array(k) { nums[start + it] }
            
            // ii. Define the target block B_i
            val B_i = Array(k) { sortedNums[start + it] }
            
            // iii. Check if A_i is a cyclic permutation of B_i
            if (!isCyclicPermutation(A_i, B_i)) {
                isSortable = false
                break
            }
        }

        // 5. If sortable, add k to sum
        if (isSortable) {
            totalSum += k.toLong()
        }
    }

    return totalSum
}
```
